The results were shown to the cheese company, and an economic and technical analysis will be made to establish the optimal option for this company.
Changes from 70 to 100% in cheese production lead to an increase of 9.25% in AP, 7.36% in EP and 7.13% in GWP, and the ADPe and HTP categories are not influenced by
Fig. 7 Sensitivity analysis variation of environmental impacts: a) ADPf and b) GWP associated with energy produc- tion scenarios
Means that do not share a letter are significantly different

these changes, the contribution is the same regardless of the allocation factor (Fig. 8a). An increase of 10% in the allocation percentage (i.e. from 70 to 80, from 80 to 90 or from 90 to 100%) resulted in increases in AP (3.08%), EP (2.45%) and GWP (2.37%). If an allocation of 20% (from 70 to 90 or 80 to 100%) is taken into account, a maximum increase of 6.17% for AP is reached. When ReCiPe method is considered, it was observed that changes from 70 to 100% in cheese production lead to an increase of 5.22% in PMF and 2.58% in TA. CC and POF impact categories were less than 1% affected by these changes, while FD and FET were not influenced at all (Fig. 8b). PMF and TA increased with 1.74% and, respectively, 0.86% when an increase of 10% in the allocation percentage was performed. A maximum increase of 3.48% is obtained when allocation of 20% is considered.
23: There are few studies upon environmental evaluation of sheep cheese production. For results comparison, the most recent LCA studies regarding sheep cheese production were selected. Also, we had selected some of the LCA studies

which investigated the production of cow milk cheese and goat cheese. The studies were chosen SO that the results obtained can be compared. In Table 3 are presented raw material, functional unit, system boundaries, LCA meth- ods and software's used for determination of environmental impacts associated with cheese production.

As functional unit, all the chosen studies considered 1 kg of cheese produced. Majority of the investigated studies included in system boundaries: dairy farm, dairy factory and transport. With the exception of two studies, the others also included wastewater treatment, and only current study included landfilling of waste.
The most used LCA software is SimaPro, followed by GaBi. It can be observed that ReCiPe LCA method, followed by CML method, was preferred by the authors for determina- tion of environmental impacts.

Energy production represents one of the important sources of environmental impacts associated with cheese production. Emissions from electricity production contribute to acidification and global warming potential by 29% and 20%, respectively, according to González-García et al. (2013b). Djekic et al. (2014) stated that electricity production from non-renewable sources contributes to climate changes through emissions of carbon dioxide. Mondello et al. (2018) consider that higher impacts are associated with electricity production. The electricity mix production, used in dairy factory for obtaining Portuguese sheep cheese, contributes with 69% to the climate change according to Nunes et al. (2020). In the current study, electricity contrib- utes to climate change with only 4.87%, due to the fact that the higher contribution is associated with the consumption of elec- tricity and diesel in the technological process. Diesel production and use contribute to climate change, and its well known that CO2, N2O and CH4 emissions are emitted during the fossil fuels burning (Nunes et al. 2020). Mondello et al. (2018) determined higher impacts associated with these activities, while Nunes et al. (2020) stated that fossil fuels burning contributes with 25% to climate change. Results obtained in current study indicate that diesel production contributes with 11.70% to this impact category. SO2 and NO, emissions are released due to the fossil energy burning for electricity production. Nunes et al. (2020) state that electricity production contributes with 94% to terres- trial acidification. Current study results indicate that electricity production contributes with 16.53% to this impact category. Wastewater treatment process contributes to climate change 1.87 kg CO2 eq per kg of cheese according to Mondello et al. (2018). Results of the current study indicated that wastewater treatment has higher contribution to freshwater eutrophication or human toxicity, than to climate change.

Particular consideration was given to the LCA methods (ReCiPe and CML) used, SO that results could be compared. In Table 4 are presented results of the current study and the results of other studies regarding environmental evaluation of cheese production obtained from sheep milk (Nunes et al.
Fig. 8 Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation: a) CML and b) ReCiPe methods
2020; Vagnoni et al. 2017) and cow milk (González-García et al. 2013b; Santos et al. 2017) as raw materials.

It can be observed that for climate change impact category the values are quite similar, when ReCiPe method is applied. The present study showed the highest value of CO2 emis- sions. The impact of cheese production from cow raw milk on the terrestrial acidification is higher than the impact of sheep cheese production. Results of the present study showed a higher value (0.030 kg P eq.) for freshwater eutrophica- tion impact comparative with the other studies. According to the results from the present study (Table 4), the emission of non-methane volatile organic compound which contrib- utes to photochemical oxidant formation is higher than the

emission reported by Santos et al. (2017). The same situation was observed also for particulate matter formation impact cat- egory. The Sb emissions value, which contributes to abiotic depletion potential elements, obtained for evaluation of Roma- nian sheep cheese is lower compared with the other studies (González-García et al. 2013b; Vagnoni et al. 2017). Results obtained by applying CML method (Table 4) showed that emission of SO2 which contributes to acidification potential is higher comparative with results obtained by González-García et al. (2013b) and Vagnoni et al. (2017). The same situation was observed for PO4 and CO2 emissions (Table 4). The results are different due to the choice of system boundaries, manufacturing technology and databases.

Diesel production and use contribute to climate change, and its well known that CO2, N2O and CH4 emissions are emitted during the fossil fuels burning (Nunes et al. 2020). Mondello et al. (2018) determined higher impacts associated with these activities, while Nunes et al. (2020) stated that fossil fuels burning contributes with 25% to climate change. Results obtained in current study indicate that diesel production contributes with 11.70% to this impact category. SO2 and NO, emissions are released due to the fossil energy burning for electricity production. Nunes et al. (2020) state that electricity production contributes with 94% to terres- trial acidification. Current study results indicate that electricity production contributes with 16.53% to this impact category. Wastewater treatment process contributes to climate change 1.87 kg CO2 eq per kg of cheese according to Mondello et al. (2018). Results of the current study indicated that wastewater treatment has higher contribution to freshwater eutrophication or human toxicity, than to climate change.

Particular consideration was given to the LCA methods (ReCiPe and CML) used, SO that results could be compared. In Table 4 are presented results of the current study and the results of other studies regarding environmental evaluation of cheese production obtained from sheep milk (Nunes et al.
Fig. 8 Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation: a) CML and b) ReCiPe methods
2020; Vagnoni et al. 2017) and cow milk (González-García et al. 2013b; Santos et al. 2017) as raw materials.

It can be observed that for climate change impact category the values are quite similar, when ReCiPe method is applied. The present study showed the highest value of CO2 emis- sions. The impact of cheese production from cow raw milk on the terrestrial acidification is higher than the impact of sheep cheese production. Results of the present study showed a higher value (0.030 kg P eq.) for freshwater eutrophica- tion impact comparative with the other studies. According to the results from the present study (Table 4), the emission of non-methane volatile organic compound which contrib- utes to photochemical oxidant formation is higher than the

emission reported by Santos et al. (2017). The same situation was observed also for particulate matter formation impact cat- egory. The Sb emissions value, which contributes to abiotic depletion potential elements, obtained for evaluation of Roma- nian sheep cheese is lower compared with the other studies (González-García et al. 2013b; Vagnoni et al. 2017). Results obtained by applying CML method (Table 4) showed that emission of SO2 which contributes to acidification potential is higher comparative with results obtained by González-García et al. (2013b) and Vagnoni et al. (2017). The same situation was observed for PO4 and CO2 emissions (Table 4). The results are different due to the choice of system boundaries, manufacturing technology and databases.

28: There are few studies upon environmental evaluation of sheep cheese production. For results comparison, the most recent LCA studies regarding sheep cheese production were selected. Also, we had selected some of the LCA studies

which investigated the production of cow milk cheese and goat cheese. The studies were chosen SO that the results obtained can be compared. In Table 3 are presented raw material, functional unit, system boundaries, LCA meth- ods and software's used for determination of environmental impacts associated with cheese production.

As functional unit, all the chosen studies considered 1 kg of cheese produced. Majority of the investigated studies included in system boundaries: dairy farm, dairy factory and transport. With the exception of two studies, the others also included wastewater treatment, and only current study included landfilling of waste.
The most used LCA software is SimaPro, followed by GaBi. It can be observed that ReCiPe LCA method, followed by CML method, was preferred by the authors for determina- tion of environmental impacts.

30: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 31: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 32: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 33: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 34: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 35: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 36: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 37: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 38: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 39: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 40: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 41: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 42: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 43: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 44: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 45: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 46: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 47: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 48: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 49: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues).
