Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it. 50: This LCA study could provide valuable insights into the potential for exploring opportunities to improve tradi- tional sheep cheese production in a small dairy industry in the mountain rural area. It was identified that diesel

utilization in the technological process of sheep cheese has higher which contributes to climate change, acidification and eutrophication. In this context, the dairy is willing to replace, where possible, a large part of the amount of diesel needed for the technological process with electric- ity, but at the same time wants to replace some part of the

energy received from the grid with energy from photovol- taic. The results of the energy production investigation|the inventory life cycle of agricultural and livestock products database. showed that the energy produced by photovoltaic panels and wind could contribute to reduction of the environ- mental impacts. The possibility of replacing energy from the mix in different percentages with renewable energy which could be produced on or close to the factory site and evaluation with LCA could be very useful in actual|Acknowledgements This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institu- tional Performance-RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. context (Romania has committed itself to increasing the amount of renewable energy used, and last but not least, the prices of both energy and fuels have increased recently, and the trend continues). Consumption of diesel during the transport phase con-|Authors' contributions CG and AL contributed with data collection and writing, specifically writing the initial version. CG and AL were the contributors with the review and editing of the manuscript. CG ana- lysed and interpreted the data used in the current study. CG was a major contributor in writing the manuscript, specifically writing the initial version and review. All authors read and approved the final manuscript. tributes to AP, GWP and EP, due to the emissions resulted from diesel combustion. In this study, different transport scenarios were investigated from environmental point of view. The distribution and sale of products only locally have been questioned, and the results have confirmed that|Funding This work was funded by Ministry of Research, Innovation and Digitalization within Program 1 Development of national research and development system, Subprogram 1.2-Institutional Performance- RDI excellence funding projects, under contract no. 10PFE/2021. this can help reduce the impact on the environment. It has been taken into account that fuel-based transport vehicles are currently being used, the trend is to switch to electric cars, and another study could be the subject of a com-|Data availability The datasets supporting the conclusions of this article are included in the article. Besides, the datasets used and/or analysed during the current study are available from the corresponding author on reasonable request. parison between the types of cars that could be used to transport dairy products. As expected, the impact on the|Declarations environment increases with the amount of diesel consumed for the distribution of the finished product. The increase in the emission which affect climate change was from 29|Ethics approval and consent to participate Not applicable. Consent for publication Not applicable. to 63% when ReCiPe method was applied, and from 53 to 82% when CML method was chosen for evaluation. There- fore, it is important to find alternatives for the transport of the finished product, more environmentally friendly. The present study indicates that the results obtained by apply- ing life cycle assessment can be further investigated using statistical methods. Tukey pairwise comparisons proved to be useful to determine whether the energy production scenarios are or not significantly different. Sensitivity of allocation factor used in cheese evaluation showed that some of the impact categories are influenced less than 10% by the changes, and some are not affected at all. The recovery of whey in the form of cheese represents envi- ronmental savings and economic benefits, compared to the consideration of whey as wastewater that would lead to increased environmental impacts and costs of treating it.
